headline: Agentic systems and test-time compute led the quarter as agents moved from chat into production pipelines.
body: Agentic systems, AI agents, and test-time compute dominated rotation this quarter, anchored by tooling that gives agents persistent state and autonomous workflows: PAI builds a Life OS on Claude Code with persistent memory and a seven-phase observe-to-learn loop [0], and PostHog is converting product signals like errors and session events into pull requests via background agents that embed LLM-generated queries for semantic clustering [3]. On the model side, Gemma 4's 31B places fourth on the LM Arena open leaderboard while running on a single GPU and on-device, underscoring continued pressure on token efficiency and foundation-model size [1]. A distinct cluster of infrastructure techniques broke out, including DeepSpeed Ulysses, Untied Ulysses, CPU offloading, and auto-scaling, alongside new entrants like percolation. Hallucination risk also surfaced concretely as lawyers were sanctioned for AI-generated citations to nonexistent cases [4].

cited_items.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
